        widgetTypeName
      • widgetTypeName(w: GestureDetector): string

GestureDetector widget plus the underlying RenderGestureDetector render object. Subscribes to pointer events that hit its subtree and fires user callbacks (onTap, onPanStart/Update/End, onDoubleTap, etc.).

The runtime.processPointerEvents proc is what drives the detector; it hit-tests through the render tree, finds the topmost detector in the hit path, and invokes handleDown / handleMove / handleUp.

Imports

../foundation/widget, ../foundation/render_object, ../foundation/geometry, ../foundation/color, ../rendering/proxy_box

Types

DragUpdate = proc (delta, position: Offset)
Drag callback. delta is the movement since the previous update; position is the absolute pointer position in window coords. 
GestureDetector = ref object of RenderObjectWidget
  child*: Widget
  onTap*: TapCallback
  onDoubleTap*: TapCallback
  onLongPress*: TapCallback
  onPanStart*: PointerCallback
  onPanUpdate*: DragUpdate
  onPanEnd*: TapCallback
  behavior*: HitTestBehavior
A widget that detects taps, double-taps, drags and other pointer gestures on its subtree. 
HitTestBehavior = enum
  htDeferToChild, htOpaque, htTranslucent
How the gesture detector participates in hit testing.
  • htDeferToChild: the detector is only hit when its child is. Pointer events that fall in padding-only areas of the detector are NOT delivered.
  • htOpaque: the detector itself is hit whenever the pointer is inside its bounds, regardless of the child. Use this for buttons so the entire box is clickable.
  • htTranslucent: the detector is hit AND any siblings behind it (in a Stack) are also tested. Currently treated as htOpaque for compatibility.
PointerCallback = proc (event: Offset)
Pointer-event handler that just needs the position. Used for onPanStart, onPointerDown, onPointerUp. 
RenderGestureDetector = ref object of RenderProxyBox
  onTap*: TapCallback
  onDoubleTap*: TapCallback
  onLongPress*: TapCallback ## NOTE: not implemented yet. Stored on the widget for
                            ## forward-compatibility; firing requires binding-level timers
                            ## not yet wired up.
  onPanStart*: PointerCallback
  onPanUpdate*: DragUpdate
  onPanEnd*: TapCallback
  onPointerDown*: PointerCallback
  onPointerUp*: PointerCallback
  behavior*: HitTestBehavior
  lastDown*: Offset
  lastTapTime*: float
  isDragging*: bool
The render object that backs GestureDetector. Stores all the callback closures plus the cross-event state needed for tap / double-tap / pan recognition. 
TapCallback = proc ()
A zero-argument click handler. Used for onTap, onDoubleTap, onPanEnd, onLongPress.

Procs

proc gestureDetector(child: Widget; onTap: TapCallback = nil;
                     onDoubleTap: TapCallback = nil;
                     onLongPress: TapCallback = nil;
                     onPanStart: PointerCallback = nil;
                     onPanUpdate: DragUpdate = nil; onPanEnd: TapCallback = nil;
                     behavior = htDeferToChild; key: Key = nil): GestureDetector {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Builds a GestureDetector around child.

Inputs:

  • child: subtree whose painted area is sensitive to gestures. Required.
  • onTap: tap-up handler. Fires if pointer-up lands within 8px of pointer-down.
  • onDoubleTap: fires when a second clean tap arrives within 300ms of the first. Consumes the second tap so onTap doesn't fire twice.
  • onLongPress: NOT YET IMPLEMENTED (see field doc).
  • onPanStart: fires once when accumulated drag exceeds 4px.
  • onPanUpdate: fires on every move during a drag; receives (delta, position).
  • onPanEnd: fires on pointer-up if we were dragging.
  • behavior: hit-test behavior. Use htOpaque for buttons so the whole padded area is tappable, not just the inner content.
  • key: optional reconciliation key.

Effect: registers a RenderGestureDetector in the render tree that the runtime's pointer dispatcher routes events to.

proc handleDown(r: RenderGestureDetector; p: Offset) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Records p as the down position and fires onPointerDown. Called by the dispatcher when a peDown lands on this detector. 
proc handleMove(r: RenderGestureDetector; p, delta: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Called for each pointer-move while a peDown is in progress. Transitions into "dragging" state once accumulated delta exceeds 4px; fires onPanStart on the transition and onPanUpdate on every move thereafter. 
proc handleUp(r: RenderGestureDetector; p: Offset) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
Fires onPointerUp, then dispatches one of:
  • onDoubleTap if a second tap-up landed within 300ms of the previous one and total travel was < 8px.
  • onTap if travel was < 8px and it's the first tap.
  • onPanEnd if we were dragging (delta moved past 4px during the gesture).

Methods

method createElement(w: GestureDetector): Element {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
method createRenderObject(w: GestureDetector; ctx: BuildContext): RenderObject {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
method hitTest(r: RenderGestureDetector; htResult: HitTestResult;
               position: Offset): bool {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Recurses into the child first. If the child reports a hit, adds this detector to the path and returns true. If the child misses and behavior == htDeferToChild, returns false (this detector is not hit). Otherwise (htOpaque / htTranslucent) the detector itself absorbs the hit even in padding-only zones. 
method updateRenderObject(w: GestureDetector; ctx: BuildContext; r: RenderObject) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
method widgetTypeName(w: GestureDetector): string {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
